title: Canonical Boundary
expectations: The PRD must clearly state that PostgreSQL remains canonical and Neo4j is derived.
It must explicitly reject Neo4j as the primary write path or source of truth.

title: Desktop Multi-Database Capability
expectations: The PRD must reflect that local Neo4j Desktop supports Enterprise-style multiple databases on this machine.
It should recommend restrained use of that capability, such as production plus staging, rather than per-source graph sprawl.

title: Cross-Database Transaction Boundary
expectations: The PRD must account for the fact that Neo4j transactions cannot span multiple databases.
It must require asynchronous projection and must not design around synchronous cross-database writes.

title: pgvector Canonical Justification
expectations: The PRD must justify keeping vectors canonical in PostgreSQL using current pgvector capability, not inertia alone.
It should stay consistent with pgvector's exact search, ANN, filtering, and hybrid-search strengths.

title: Neo4j Vector Capability Boundary
expectations: The PRD must acknowledge that Neo4j now has native vector indexes for nodes and relationships.
It must still explain why Neo4j vectors are not the default v1 architecture.

title: Neo4j Vector Operational Cost
expectations: The PRD must reflect the real operational cost of Neo4j vector indexes, especially OS filesystem-cache pressure.
It should not treat Neo4j vector duplication as free.

title: Provenance-First Scope
expectations: Phase 1 must be provenance-first and must not depend on fuzzy entity extraction.
The rollout must not begin with an unconstrained graph-of-everything.

title: Confidence Discipline
expectations: The PRD must distinguish deterministic provenance links from LLM-extracted entity links.
LLM-derived nodes or edges must carry confidence and evidence.

title: Official GraphRAG Scope
expectations: The PRD must account for the existence of Neo4j's official GraphRAG package.
It should treat that package as an implementation reference rather than letting it silently define the architecture.

title: Retrieval Integration
expectations: The PRD must keep PostgreSQL semantic retrieval as the default path.
Graph-assisted retrieval must be optional and evaluated, not silently replace vector retrieval.

title: Evaluation Plan
expectations: The PRD must include concrete product questions or evaluation scenarios where graph should help.
Success criteria must protect grounded-answer discipline, not just graph completeness.

title: Implementation Readiness
expectations: The PRD must define enough initial node labels and relationship types to implement Phase 1.
It must also define projection state or equivalent operational bookkeeping.
